super_eco.title: SUPER ECO
super_eco.description: Super ECO pushes efficiency even further than ECO for the lowest power draw and noise. Available on Apollo III only.

eco.title: ECO
eco.description: In ECO mode your miner will be at its most efficient, but its hashrate will be slightly slower. This mode is recommended, and will produce the least amount of noise and heat.

balanced.title: BALANCED
balanced.description: BALANCED mode is a good compromise between hashrate, efficiency, and noise.

turbo.title: TURBO
turbo.description: In TURBO mode your miner will be the least efficient, but its hashrate will be the highest. This mode is only recommended for expert users, and you should monitor your miner for possible overheating. The fan can get loud in this mode.

custom.title: CUSTOM
custom.description: In CUSTOM mode you can set your own frequency and voltage. This mode is only recommended for expert users.
custom.warning_description: The Apollo comes with tuned preset values (above), which offer a good range of operating modes. By selecting custom you risk damaging your device and FutureBit will not be responsible for any or all damage caused by over-clocking or over-volting

power.title: Power
power.description: You can set your miner custom power or reset to default value.

frequency.title: Frequency
frequency.description: You can set your miner custom frequency or reset to default value.

fan.title: Miner fan settings
fan.description: Adjust the fan speed or set it to automatic

low.title: Temperature to start fan
low.description: This is the temperature needed to start the fan.

high.title: Temperature for max fan speed
high.description: This is the temperature needed to set the fan at maximum speed.

power_led.title: Front Status Light
power_led.description: Turn off/on the front status led. Note: your miner will be restarted to apply.

node.title: Bitcoin node settings

software.title: Node Software
software.description: Choose between Bitcoin Core and Bitcoin Knots

tor_mode.title: TOR mode
tor_mode.description: Connect your Bitcoin Node over the Tor network to increase security and anonymity. Note: your node will be restarted to apply.

allow_lan.title: Allow LAN connections
allow_lan.description: Allow LAN connections to your Bitcoin Node to connect external wallets and services. Note: your node will be restarted to apply.

node_config.title: Bitcoin node configuration
node_config.description: Add additional configuration lines to the bitcoin.conf file. (Note: this section is for advanced users, and no validation is performed. Please check Bitcoin Core documentation for valid options.)

node.solo_mining_warning: You have solo mining enabled, it is suggested to turn off tor for solo mining. Bitcoin nodes over the tor network propagate blocks slower, and there is a higher chance of orphaning a block

scan.title: Scan WiFi Networks
scan.description: Click the scan button and your system will scan for available wifi networks. Click one of the available SSIDs, and enter your WiFi passphrase. Clicking the disconnect button will delete all saved WiFi connections. If you are having issues connecting, set your wifi router to a seperate 2.4GHz SSID with WPA2 only security.

password.title: Lockscreen
password.description: Change the password to access the dashboard

error.required: The password must have 8 characters at least

logs.title: System Logs
logs.description: View and monitor system logs for different components

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: The device clock label. Wrong here, and log timestamps, charts and time-based automation rules all drift.

location.title: Location
location.description: Latitude and longitude, used by the automation to compute sunrise and sunset locally.
location.hint: Search a city to fill the coordinates, or type them by hand. Never inferred from your IP. Leave empty if you do not use sun-based rules.
location.search_failed: Could not reach the location service. Enter the coordinates by hand.

backup.title: Backup settings

restore.title: Restore settings
restore.description: Restore all configurations from a backup file

format.description: Use this tool for formatting and setting up your Node NVMe SSD

pool.description: Manage pools configuration for your miner

backup.title: Backup pool
backup.description: If the primary pool becomes unreachable, the miner will failover to this backup.

solo.title: SOLO settings
solo.description: Mine directly to your Bitcoin node
solo.mining_mode_description: Enable solo mining mode to mine directly to your own Bitcoin Node. Note: your node will be restarted to apply.
solo.wallet_info: Enter your wallet address for solo mining payout. This is the address the full block reward will go to if you find a block. Do NOT use an exchange address, and only use a Bitcoin address you fully control. If your unit came with a FutureBit Satscard, you can tap the card on an NFC enabled phone to view the payout address, and copy and paste the address below.
solo.wallet_placeholder: ENTER BITCOIN WALLET PAYOUT ADDRESS HERE
solo.tor_warning: You have tor enabled, it is suggested to turn off tor for solo mining. Bitcoin nodes over the tor network propagate blocks slower, and there is a higher chance of orphaning a block
solo.node_not_synced: Your Bitcoin node is not running or not fully synced. You can enable solo mining only after your node is fully synced.
solo.btc_signature_info: This is the block signature displayed if you find a solo block. You can leave as default, or tag it with your own message/signature for the world to see!
solo.startdiff_info: Modify the start difficulty value for miners connecting to Solo Pool, this is useful for really low or really high hashrate
solo.mindiff_info: Set a higher minimum difficulty to force var diff to not fall below this level, useful for rental/high hashpower to keep shares per second low
solo.invalid_address: Please add a valid Bitcoin address
solo.incompatible_address: Warning: P2WSH and P2TR Bitcoin address are not valid for SOLO mining. Please add a different Bitcoin address

block_found.message: Your miner has found a Bitcoin block!
block_found.description: This is an extraordinary achievement in the Bitcoin mining world. You have successfully mined an entire block and earned the full reward!

signin.title: Welcome to Apollo Web OS 2
signin.heading: Sign In
signin.description: Enter your password to sign in!

welcome.title: One node. One miner. One person.
welcome.subtitle: Welcome.

password.title: Set lock and system password
password.too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters long

solo_warning.title: Solo Mining Requires Synced Node
solo_warning.description: Solo mining can only be used once your node is fully synced. You can configure it now, but mining will only start when the node sync is complete.

solo.title: Solo Mining
solo.description: You are competing with the entire network for a full Bitcoin Block (the whole BTC reward). You gain zero rewards while mining, in hopes of hitting the entire block. This is also called "lottery" mining because the chances are low, but this produces the maximum decentralization of the bitcoin network. If there are millions of solo miners then solo blocks will be found every day and ensure Bitcoin can never be censored!

pool.title: Pooled Mining
pool.description: You are contributing your hashrate to a collective "pool" that is competing to find a block. This produces daily rewards in mined "satoshis" because once your pool finds a block, it shares that block reward with you based on your % of hashrate contributed to the pool. These is the least decentralized form of mining, because you give control of block creation to the pool. Please note most pools have minimum payouts, that can take months of mining before you accumulate enough for a payout.

disclaimer.title: Legal Disclaimer for Solo Mining
disclaimer.part1: The Apollo II offers enhanced features for solo mining, including the ability to launch your own Stratum solo pool with a simple toggle in the user interface. While these features are designed for ease of use and efficiency, solo mining inherently carries certain risks that users must be aware of. Specifically, the process of solo mining is sensitive to network conditions. Factors such as Bitcoin network congestion, network latency and upload speeds can impact the successful submission of a solo block to the Bitcoin network.
disclaimer.part2: Due to the decentralized and competitive nature of the Bitcoin network, a block mined by the user may be orphaned and not accepted by the network, especially in cases of high network latency or suboptimal connection speeds. While FutureBit has engineered the Apollo II to minimize such risks at a device level, the outcome of solo mining activities is influenced by factors beyond our control.
disclaimer.part3: By using the Apollo II for solo mining, users acknowledge and accept that FutureBit is not responsible for any lost or orphaned solo blocks that may occur as a result of hardware issues, software issues, network conditions including but not limited to latency and connection stability. FutureBit disclaims any liability for losses or damages resulting from solo mining activities. Users engage in solo mining at their own risk and are advised to ensure a high-speed, stable Ethernet connection for optimal performance.

wallet.title: Set wallet address
wallet.description: Enter your wallet address for solo mining payout. This is the address the full block reward will go to if you find a block. Do NOT use an exchange address, and only use a Bitcoin address you fully control. If your unit came with a FutureBit Satscard, you can tap the card on an NFC enable phone to view the payout address, and copy and paste the address below. Please note you will not start solo mining until your node is fully synced, which can take several days.
wallet.address_placeholder: ENTER BITCOIN WALLET PAYOUT ADDRESS HERE

error.invalid_address: Please add a valid Bitcoin address
error.incompatible_address: P2WSH and P2TR Bitcoin addresses are not valid for solo mining

reboot.confirm_description: Are you sure you want to reboot the system? All services will be temporarily unavailable.
reboot.loading_description: Please wait while the system reboots. This will take approximately {seconds} seconds.

shutdown.confirm_description: Are you sure you want to shutdown the system? You will need to manually restart the device.
shutdown.loading_description: The system is shutting down. Please wait {seconds} seconds.
shutdown.complete_description: The system has been shut down. You can safely disconnect the power.

automation.title: Automation

status.title: Automation
status.description: Rules decide when the miner runs, and at which power level.
status.dry_run.tooltip: Evaluate the rules and record every decision, without ever touching the miner.
status.dry_run.banner: Observation mode: decisions are recorded but the miner is not touched. Turn this off when you trust the rules.
status.paused: Automation paused until {time} — you took manual control.

rules.title: Rules
rules.description: Checked top to bottom — the first rule that matches wins. Drag to reorder.
rules.empty: No rules yet. Add one to let the automation act.

editor.name: Name
editor.safety_tooltip: A safety rule runs even while the automation is paused, and bypasses the guard rails. Use it for overheating.
editor.hysteresis_tooltip: Keeps the threshold sticky while this rule is in charge: stop above X, resume only below X minus the hysteresis. Without it a value hovering on the threshold would cycle the miner.
editor.mode_restart_warning: Changing mode restarts the miner and costs a few minutes of downtime.
editor.running_only_warning: {signals} can only be read while the miner runs, so this rule could never turn it back on. Use it to stop the miner (over-temperature) instead, and turn the miner on with a signal available while off — time, sunrise/sunset, or outdoor temperature.

guards.title: Guard rails
guards.description: Miners dislike fast cycling, and a mode change restarts them. These limits keep a noisy signal from wearing out the hardware.
guards.minOnMinutes.tooltip: Once started, the miner stays on at least this long.
guards.minOffMinutes.tooltip: Once stopped, the miner stays off at least this long.
guards.minChangeMinutes.tooltip: Minimum interval between two changes of any kind, including mode changes.
guards.maxCyclesPerHour.tooltip: How many times the miner may be started within one hour.
guards.defaultHysteresis.tooltip: Applied to numeric thresholds when a rule does not set its own.
guards.overrideMinutes.tooltip: How long the automation stays paused after you start or stop the miner by hand.
guards.fallback.tooltip: What to do when no rule applies. "Leave as is" never touches the miner.

tariff.title: Energy cost
tariff.description: Entered by hand: there is no worldwide price API. Rules can then act on the price.
tariff.no_days_error: Each time band needs at least one day selected.
tariff.time_error: Each band needs a valid start and end time (HH:mm).

events.title: History
events.description: What the engine did, and the values behind it.

automation.location_alert: A rule uses sunrise/sunset but no location is set, so it can never match. {link}
automation.location_alert_link: Set the location in Settings → System

signal_hint.clock.time: Based on the time of day.
signal_hint.clock.weekday: Based on the day of the week.
signal_hint.clock.date: Based on a specific calendar date.
signal_hint.sun.isDay: True while the sun is up at your location. Needs a location set in Settings.
signal_hint.sun.minutesToSunset: Minutes left until sunset. Needs a location set in Settings.
signal_hint.sun.minutesToSunrise: Minutes left until sunrise. Needs a location set in Settings.
signal_hint.miner.temperature: The hottest board temperature right now.
signal_hint.miner.running: Whether the miner is currently on.
signal_hint.miner.mode: The miner’s current power mode. Most useful combined with another condition.
signal_hint.energy.price: The energy price active right now, taken from the tariff below.
signal_hint.energy.band: The name of the price band active right now.
signal_hint.weather.temperature: The outdoor air temperature at your location (Open-Meteo). Needs a location set in Settings.
signal_hint.weather.cloudCover: How overcast it is now, 0–100% (Open-Meteo). Lower is sunnier.
signal_hint.weather.solarRadiation: Current solar radiation in W/m² (Open-Meteo). Higher means more sun for your panels.
signal_hint.mqtt_input: A value read from an MQTT topic (Home Assistant). Stale while the broker is disconnected.

mqtt.title: MQTT / Home Assistant
mqtt.description: Connect to your MQTT broker: read topics as signals, and publish this device to Home Assistant.
mqtt.inputs_hint: Map a topic to a signal (e.g. name “surplus”, topic “sun2000/surplus”) to use it in rules.
mqtt.name: Signal name
mqtt.output_hint: Publish this device to the broker so it appears in Home Assistant (MQTT Discovery).
mqtt.output_control_hint: Expose a switch and mode select so Home Assistant can start, stop and set the miner.
mqtt.browse_value_hint: The scan listens for up to a minute. Retained values show at once, but a topic that only publishes on an interval — e.g. a solar bridge every ~40s, not retained — needs the wait to be caught.
mqtt.browse_none: No topics seen in the scan window. Try again or a different prefix.
mqtt.inputs_desc: Map broker topics to signals your rules can react to.
mqtt.broker_hint: Set up the MQTT broker first in {link}.

setup.hint: Set these once — they apply to all your rules. The tariff bands you define here become usable as an energy.band condition.

conditions.title: Current conditions
conditions.location_hint: Sun and weather conditions need your location. {link} to enable them.

templates.title: Rule templates
templates.subtitle: Pick a starting point, then set your own thresholds. Each opens in the editor before it is saved.

thermal_protection.name: Thermal protection
thermal_protection.desc: Stop the miner when the board gets too hot. It runs again once your other rules do, after it has cooled.

workday_pause.name: Workday pause
workday_pause.desc: Turn off during working hours on weekdays (Mon–Fri, 09:00–18:00).

daytime_turbo.name: Daytime turbo
daytime_turbo.desc: Run in turbo while the sun is up. Needs your location.

sunny_mild_turbo.name: Sunny & mild → turbo
sunny_mild_turbo.desc: Run in turbo when it is daytime and not too hot outside. Needs your location.

backend_offline.title: Backend offline
backend_offline.description: Cannot reach the Apollo API service. Check that the backend is running and that your device is on the same network.
backend_offline.tip_service: Verify the Apollo API service is running
backend_offline.tip_network: Check that your device is connected to the local network
